112747.explanation: As residents, we govern our own municipality. When an issue plays a major role, a referendum can be a fair and effective way to make a decision. That way, we involve people in local democracy.

112748.explanation: When you give people the trust to build something together in their own neighborhood, wonderful things emerge. The municipality should not stop this but rather encourage it.

112750.explanation: To balance liveliness and livability, opening hours should not be completely free. Distinctions must be made between entertainment areas with more freedoms and residential areas with more rules.

112751.explanation: We take our responsibility and cooperate in a fair distribution of refugees and asylum seekers in solidarity with other municipalities and residents of the Netherlands. Our commitment remains humane at all times.

112755.explanation: Garbage, old mattresses, and other litter on the street are annoying, unsafe, and unhealthy. The easiest way to prevent this is by collecting bulky waste for free. This avoids hassle and gives us cleaner streets.

112756.explanation: We strive for a good balance between liveliness and livability: allowing space for festivals and parties in a way that causes as little nuisance as possible for people and nature.

112758.explanation: Sports give a lot of energy and satisfaction. And it keeps you healthy. But for people with welfare benefits, sports are often too expensive. We believe the municipality should ensure this group can participate in a sports club for free.

112761.explanation: Children are under huge pressure, from addictive social media to performance pressure at school. Music is the perfect outlet. We want music lessons not only to be accessible to children with wealthy parents. This way, we offer every child equal opportunities.

112765.explanation: Feeling safe on the street is very important. But we do not think more cameras are the right solution. Your privacy is a great good that should not be infringed lightly. Therefore, we prefer more enforcement officers on the streets who can intervene immediately if something goes wrong.

112766.explanation: The monopoly on violence lies with the police. Enforcement officers should maintain close contact with the police so that assistance can be provided quickly when needed.

112769.explanation: We want housing for everyone and to combat homelessness. This also includes exploring alternative housing concepts.

112771.explanation: People are not on welfare because they don’t want to work. They need a stable foundation, affordable housing, and trust from the municipality. Extra demands and conditions only create more uncertainty and cause new problems.

112772.explanation: If the exemption of municipal taxes stops, people with the lowest incomes in our municipality will get into serious trouble. They simply cannot spare that money. We believe everyone should be able to keep their head above water; therefore, we support tax exemption.

112775.explanation: A basic income is the foundation for security of existence and thus the eradication of poverty.

112776.explanation: With the continuous rise of electric cars, there must also be more charging spots. The municipality is committed to low charging fees to make electric driving feasible for more and more people.

112785.explanation: With the development of Distripark and the Third Merwedehaven, the business parks have already been expanded. Current developments fit within these sites. We focus on sustainable businesses with long-term jobs that have an impact on our residents.

112786.explanation: All Dordrecht residents have the right to housing. Various groups without housing (including victims of domestic violence, ex-prisoners, status holders) must be allocated a home by the municipality.

112788.explanation: Farmers should have more say in how they want to use their land – for agriculture, housing, or contributing to sustainable energy. It is important that solar panels, which can also be mounted on poles with crops and animals around them, are well integrated into the landscape.

112791.explanation: 30% of new build homes will be realized as social rental housing, and the aim is to also build 30% affordable owner-occupied homes.

112794.explanation: Due to enormous congestion, parking fees are unavoidable in certain locations to regulate traffic. Additionally, parking fees encourage healthy alternatives like cycling and more climate-friendly public transport.

112795.explanation: Neighborhoods need space not only for parking but also for greenery, recreation, and social encounters. Adding parking at the edge of a neighborhood or district remains a possibility.

112796.explanation: It is important that necessary transport, shop deliveries, and people with disabilities can still come by car to the city center. But to enhance the livability of our historic city, we want to expand car-free zones.

112797.explanation: New construction in the city center and other neighborhoods is limited. Preserving green and public spaces is crucial for the residents of Dordrecht. There simply isn't enough space in the city to fully meet the needed housing demand.

112799.explanation: Coffeeshops should not be concentrated in one place where the chance of nuisance is high. Distribution across neighborhoods is important. All coffeeshops must have a license and comply with rules to minimize nuisance to the neighborhood.

112800.explanation: It is crucial that the Albert Schweitzer Hospital is easily accessible. A location close to the current site is therefore desirable.

112801.explanation: We focus on free public transport for people with low incomes, regardless of their age.

112803.explanation: We want to focus on reducing waste and improving waste separation (pre-sorting) and recycling.

112805.explanation: Sport is important for everyone. We want to get as many people as possible involved in sports and therefore invest in grassroots sports. Top-level sports that develop from grassroots sports can be further financed by the business community.

112747.explanation: The Dordrecht VVD attaches great importance to input from residents and entrepreneurs through participation. We see no reason to change the current referendum options in the municipality.

112748.explanation: The Dordrecht VVD believes in the power of local initiatives. We support taking responsibility and good initiatives in the city.

112750.explanation: Our (inner) city could be a lot cosier. The cafes and restaurants make an important contribution to that.

112751.explanation: The national distribution law obliges Dordrecht to open an asylum seeker center (azc). Agreements have been made about safety around the azc and they must be adhered to. If not, the azc can and should be closed in our view. The azc is temporary, so no extension of its duration.

112755.explanation: User-friendly services around waste processing are an important principle for the Dordrecht VVD. We all pay waste disposal fees for this. Besides free bulky waste drop-off, we believe home collection should also be an option.

112756.explanation: Events in Dordrecht provide a lot of enjoyment and sociability. This happens in a good balance between liveliness and livability. The Dordrecht VVD therefore sees no reason to tighten noise standards. That would reduce the availability of events.

112758.explanation: We find healthy and sociable sports very important. Therefore, the threshold for sports club membership must be low, also for Dordrecht residents who work hard for a minimum income. We want to avoid poverty traps when moving from welfare to work.

112761.explanation: Music is fun and a proven enrichment for children's development. The Dordrecht VVD wants to promote this.

112765.explanation: The Dordrecht VVD aims to firmly tackle antisocial and criminal behavior in Dordrecht. Camera surveillance, noise speed cameras, and extra police checks are all on our wishlist to make our city’s streets safer and cleaner.

112766.explanation: For the Dordrecht VVD, safety is paramount: both for residents and enforcement officers (boa’s). The municipality must also invest in boa training so deployment is responsible.

112769.explanation: There is a great need for housing, so every opportunity for new homes must be seized. If there are vacant offices that can no longer be used as offices, we support their transformation into homes. We do monitor critically to ensure enough office space remains.

112771.explanation: The Dordrecht VVD finds it important that everyone contributes actively according to their ability.

112772.explanation: Costs such as waste processing must be paid by everyone. Dordrecht currently grants much more fee remission than other municipalities. We want to prevent that working is not rewarding because there is too much compensation in welfare situations.

112775.explanation: This is income policy, which is the responsibility of the national government, not the municipality. The Dordrecht VVD has the liberal conviction that participation and work must be rewarding; a basic income does not stimulate that.

112776.explanation: The Dordrecht VVD finds it important that there is sufficient parking space in neighborhoods for everyone. That is why we have also worked to make home charging of electric cars easier when you don’t have your own driveway.

112781.explanation: There is already great pressure on healthcare and often staff shortages. These kinds of extra conditions increase healthcare costs too much and add too much pressure on care.

112785.explanation: Space is scarce and we already have many business parks in our city. The Dordrecht VVD focuses on better utilization. We invest to allow more companies to establish on the same site and to make waterfront business locations also available for the maritime sector.

112786.explanation: Housing pressure for regular home seekers is already high. By abolishing this priority rule, residents with a strong bond to Dordrecht also have a better chance to continue living in their own city.

112788.explanation: Solar panels on all roofs, not in the polders. Our motto is 'roofs first' for solar energy. We first make use of all suitable roofs for panels.

112791.explanation: There is a great need for all types of homes and high demand for affordable owner-occupied homes. The Dordrecht VVD focuses on rapid realization of all housing plans and opposes piling on requirements that cause delays.

112794.explanation: Paid parking is a tool to properly spread parking pressure so that parking spaces near your home remain available. The fee for residents’ parking permits should therefore be moderate. Paid parking is a service for residents struggling with high parking pressure, not a cash cow.

112795.explanation: In various neighborhoods, people depend on a car for-for example-work or taking and fetching children. That also requires space for a second car. Where needed, the Dordrecht VVD wants to invest in extra parking spaces.

112796.explanation: We support a good balance between an accessible and attractive (green) city center. A larger (than planned) car-free center would harm accessibility and thus reduce visitation needed for a lively inner city.

112797.explanation: We want to build near existing facilities, so within the city, also for seniors living in Dubbeldam. This way we can continue enjoying our Biesbosch and the open polder landscape at the same time.

112799.explanation: The city center has too many coffeeshops causing considerable (traffic) nuisance. Therefore, the Dordrecht VVD favors relocating them to better accessible places without nuisance.

112800.explanation: The hospital is an important facility and employer for the city, and we want to keep it within Dordrecht and in a central location. It does not have to be in the exact same place.

112801.explanation: Through a general measure like free public transport for those 65 and older, we have less money left for important tailored transport such as the Wijkhopper. The Dordrecht VVD finds it important that all people aged 65 and over remain as mobile as possible and thus can receive tailored support in this.

112802.explanation: The Dordrecht VVD finds it important that shopkeepers have the freedom to decide themselves when they are open or closed.

112803.explanation: The Dordrecht VVD finds it important that waste is collected in a user-friendly and cost-efficient manner. Sorting is, for example, difficult in high-rise buildings. Post-separation at the waste processor can also be a good solution.

112805.explanation: At the Krommedijk they provide enjoyable evenings, connection, and top sports. The club has shown more social commitment. We want to continue supporting that and funds have already been reserved. A professional football club is a commercial organization and must therefore be financially self-sufficient.

112747.explanation: Beter Voor Dordt does not think a referendum should be impossible. But there are many other ways to hear the opinions of residents. There may be circumstances or topics where a referendum can ultimately be a good supplementary instrument.

112748.explanation: When it comes to managing daily usage, that should be possible with good agreements.

112750.explanation: There are national rules. We impose as few additional restrictions as possible but always keep an eye on livability.

112751.explanation: Beter Voor Dordt is not against refugee shelter. There was no consultation for the current shelter location. The size of the current location is far too large-scale for the area.

112755.explanation: As far as Beter Voor Dordt is concerned, this is included in the annual costs that residents pay.

112758.explanation: For children, we have the Dordt Pass in Dordrecht. For adults, we can encourage the use of public sports facilities.

112761.explanation: Children whose parents have a low income can apply for the Dordt Pass.

112765.explanation: Focused on the safety of the citizen and the environment.

112766.explanation: Society is becoming tougher and enforcement officers must be able to defend themselves.

112769.explanation: It doesn't always have to be through subsidies; it can also be through support with permit issuance.

112771.explanation: It is about work that suits the individual,

112772.explanation: It probably causes more hassle than savings.

112775.explanation: According to Beter Voor Dordt, this is not something we should solve locally.

112776.explanation: It must grow in relation to the number of electric cars. We stimulate home charging and flexible charging spots.

112781.explanation: The initiative for this does not lie with the municipality.

112785.explanation: We still have sufficient free plots.

112786.explanation: We disagree with housing refugees in the homes for which Dordrecht residents have been on the waiting list for years. We believe we must meet the government's mandate to house refugees, but this must also be possible in creative ways, such as distributed throughout the city in vacant buildings, small flexible units, or grouped in small clusters.

112788.explanation: The scarce agricultural land must remain available for agriculture.

112791.explanation: It is not always possible per project, but citywide we maintain at least 30%.

112794.explanation: Parking for residents and local entrepreneurs must remain affordable.

112795.explanation: As far as Beter Voor Dordt is concerned, this does not come at the expense of green areas but we choose smart solutions.

112796.explanation: No more expansion than the plans that already exist.

112797.explanation: We keep our polders green! There are enough possibilities within the city boundaries.

112799.explanation: To relieve residents and entrepreneurs in the city center.

112800.explanation: At least in the same area.

112801.explanation: Was a success until 2022! It promotes the mobility of seniors.

112802.explanation: No unnecessary restrictions; it is the free choice of the entrepreneur.

112803.explanation: Beter Voor Dordt supports post-separation sorting.

112805.explanation: Beter Voor Dordt does believe the municipality should think constructively and provide support.

112747.explanation: The VSP is in favor of a corrective referendum.

112748.explanation: It is possible, but we want to assess this per situation considering support, finances, and guarantees.

112750.explanation: Cafés can cause nuisance, which must be curbed. However, they should be allowed to stay open a few days longer.

112751.explanation: The VSP is against an asylum seeker center (azc). We will do everything to stop this, and if that is not possible, we will certainly try to halve it.

112755.explanation: At least four times a year.

112756.explanation: Dordrecht is an event city and we believe it should remain so. There is always some noise nuisance during events. Current noise standards are sufficient.

112758.explanation: Everyone should be able to play sports. It is healthy and prevents discomfort and diseases in the long term.

112761.explanation: The VSP believes music is good for the general development of children.

112765.explanation: The VSP believes that in places where demonstrably dangerous situations or livability are at risk, there should be more camera surveillance.

112766.explanation: Provided that the public enforcement officers (boa's) are well trained and educated. The VSP also definitely advocates for more police presence on the streets because safety is a core task of the police.

112769.explanation: The VSP believes an active policy should be pursued to convert office buildings into homes, especially considering the housing shortage in Dordrecht.

112771.explanation: It can promote return to the labor market. Customized solutions should be carefully considered. We must not be flogging a dead horse as this costs the community more money than it yields.

112772.explanation: The current arrangement should be maintained.

112775.explanation: Having a social assistance benefit is already a kind of basic support.

112776.explanation: Home charging (of electric cars) should be made easier. Unnecessary restrictions should not be maintained.

112781.explanation: Care is care. The VSP values the quality of care more. Everyone who needs care should be able to receive it.

112785.explanation: Let's first ensure that existing business parks are filled.

112786.explanation: The VSP believes that residents of Dordrecht should have priority.

112788.explanation: Let the agricultural land be used for its intended purpose. Place solar panels first on all government buildings.

112791.explanation: The VSP even wants to go a step further here to 35% social housing because in recent years only expensive housing has been built.

112794.explanation: It makes the city more attractive for visitors and entrepreneurs.

112795.explanation: There are households where children still live at home out of necessity but also own a car. More parking spaces prevent double parking and parking chaos.

112796.explanation: Zero emission solves itself over time because more and more cars run on hydrogen or electricity. Postponement can save a lot of money and annoyance for entrepreneurs and market traders.

112797.explanation: Keep our polders green. They are the green lung of our island.

112799.explanation: We would prefer to move the coffee shop to, for example, industrial areas to reduce nuisance in the city center. Think of a 'coffee shop to go'.

112800.explanation: The Albert Schweitzer Hospital is one of the largest employers in our city. In good consultation, find a suitable location together so we can keep the hospital for our city. It ensures employment and care close to home.

112801.explanation: The VSP supports free bus travel for those 65 and older.

112802.explanation: The VSP considers entrepreneurial freedom very important.

112803.explanation: The VSP supports post-separation (sorting waste after disposal). It is cheaper and prevents illegal dumping.

112805.explanation: The VSP believes FC Dordrecht is very important for our city. We want to preserve it. However, FC Dordt is a professional football club and thus a commercial company. The municipality must do everything to properly arrange infrastructure and permits for the club.

112747.explanation: Disagree, but we are in favor of other instruments such as a citizens' assembly.

112748.explanation: Agree, it fits with the right to challenge.

112750.explanation: No, it is undesirable to release this due to livability concerns.

112751.explanation: We feel it is our responsibility to contribute to asylum reception.

112755.explanation: We must especially continue to facilitate well for residents.

112756.explanation: Festivals and parties should be possible in our city, but within limits so that the environment remains liveable.

112758.explanation: Disagree. There are many options available for children via the Dordtpass. Beyond that, we encourage outdoor exercise for everyone.

112761.explanation: Parents with low incomes can receive support through the Dordtpass. We want to maintain this.

112765.explanation: Agree, where it significantly contributes to increased safety.

112766.explanation: Support. Public enforcement officers (boa's) must be able to act and defend themselves when necessary.

112769.explanation: Where possible, converting vacant offices into homes is a good investment.

112771.explanation: It is good to encourage people to volunteer wherever possible. Good for the city and for the people themselves. Obligations have little demonstrable effect. For that reason, we should avoid imposing them to prevent unnecessarily burdening people.

112772.explanation: Debt remission is necessary for some people.

112775.explanation: A large and costly experiment with little certainty.

112776.explanation: Not necessarily. Residents and businesses can submit requests. The municipality facilitates.

112781.explanation: Absolutely. It is very important that residents can make their own choices in this.

112785.explanation: Yes, but only in certain areas, such as Dordtse Kil.

112786.explanation: Agreed, we must fulfill our obligation. Every municipality must take responsibility in this.

112788.explanation: Disagree. In some locations this is fine. However, we do find it important that we also make maximum use of roofs for solar panels.

112791.explanation: We desperately need enough social rental housing.

112794.explanation: We want to discourage car traffic in the city center. In other neighborhoods, parking fees must remain cost-covering.

112795.explanation: When we increase density, more parking spaces will be needed in some places. For new residential neighborhoods, we aim for a lower parking standard.

112796.explanation: We are in favor of a low-car historic city center.

112797.explanation: Disagree. We want to focus on densification to keep our green polders green.

112799.explanation: Ideally, we would like the coffeeshop to disappear completely, but where they do exist, we prefer them to be located in the industrial area.

112800.explanation: We prefer to wait for the plans first.

112801.explanation: Disagree. Not every person over 65 needs to travel free by public transport. The current scheme already offers a lot of support to those who need it.

112802.explanation: We do not want an extension of shopping hours. In the latest support survey, the shopkeepers themselves mostly indicated they have no need for this.

112803.explanation: The better the waste is separated beforehand, the lower the waste levy.

112805.explanation: This is the responsibility of the association itself.

112747.explanation: Most important topics are too complex for a referendum. Good participation is crucial for proper decision-making.

112748.explanation: This is organized by halls managed by associations (such as the Oranje Wit Hall, for example).

112750.explanation: Opening hours must be regulated to safeguard the livability of the environment.

112751.explanation: Every municipality must take responsibility. This keeps it bearable for each municipality, including Dordrecht.

112755.explanation: You may have bulky waste collected free of charge a few times a year.

112756.explanation: The current noise standards are sufficient.

112758.explanation: We arrange this through the Dordtpas.

112761.explanation: Music lessons are part of the standard curriculum.

112765.explanation: Everyone in Dordrecht can live, work, and go out safely. Camera surveillance helps with this.

112766.explanation: Provided the enforcement officers are authorized to use a baton.

112769.explanation: Often money is not the problem but changes to the zoning plan. The municipality should show flexibility here.

112771.explanation: This is already done as much as possible and we must continue. This is the implementation of the Participation Act.

112772.explanation: This must remain part of our generous poverty policy.

112775.explanation: We leave this to the national government.

112776.explanation: This should move along with the number of electric cars and not the other way around.

112781.explanation: Only if the contracted care provider is involved and the municipality must ensure sufficient diversity in this.

112785.explanation: The Dordtse Kil is the last major expansion location.

112788.explanation: Land unsuitable for other purposes can be used for solar energy generation, but only if residents can participate in it.

112791.explanation: The starting point for new construction projects is that 30% consists of social rental housing.

112794.explanation: For residents of neighborhoods with paid parking, we aim to let the cost of the first permit rise no more than inflation.

112795.explanation: The existing parking spaces must remain available to the residents of those neighborhoods.

112796.explanation: Only the Bagijnhof could possibly be added if the Visstraat parking garage becomes resident parking.

112797.explanation: For now, there is enough space for housing within the built-up area. For the CDA, housing in the polder is only discussable at the edges where it currently looks untidy.

112799.explanation: The CDA does not want coffeeshops in residential neighborhoods.

112800.explanation: Although this does not have to be exactly the same location, it must be in the same area.

112801.explanation: Bus transportation in Dordrecht must remain free for people over 65 with a minimum income.

112802.explanation: This measure affects small retailers. We believe that the current regulation (allowing stores to open at 12 noon) is sufficient.

112803.explanation: Separated waste must lead to effective reuse.

112805.explanation: FC Dordrecht is a commercial organization where we must observe state aid rules.

112747.explanation: The advisory referendum, as far as the Party for the Animals is concerned, should have a place with important decisions, such as major construction projects or municipal reorganization. We also enable neighborhood referendums.

112748.explanation: The Party for the Animals focuses on strengthening local initiatives and resident groups. They are actively involved in decisions about the neighborhood and can participate in implementation and maintenance through the right to challenge.

112750.explanation: We want a vibrant city that is also liveable. Nightlife is part of that. However, unlimited opening hours for all cafes and restaurants go too far.

112751.explanation: Dordrecht will be a place where all people, regardless of residence status or papers, have a right to humane treatment and safe housing. We oppose scapegoat politics that pit residents and refugees against each other.

112755.explanation: The collection or drop-off of bulky waste is free up to a certain amount.

112756.explanation: Both animals and residents can suffer greatly from festivals and large events. Therefore, the municipality sets strict rules to counteract noise and light pollution. In addition, we believe nature reserves are not suitable locations for festivals.

112758.explanation: The municipality encourages participation in sports, especially among groups that participate little, including (children of) people with low incomes. They can receive a discount on their subscription via the Dordtpass.

112761.explanation: Good education includes not only cognitive skills but also development in social, emotional, motor, and creative abilities. The municipality invests in sports, swimming lessons, culture, music lessons, and (independent) nature and sustainability education in schools.

112765.explanation: Municipal camera surveillance is handled very cautiously due to privacy considerations. It may only be temporary and under strict conditions, such as a maximum retention period for the footage.

112766.explanation: BOAs (public order officers) are not police officers. The police have the monopoly on violence. Officers receive appropriate training for using force. BOAs have different responsibilities and training, which also requires different equipment.

112769.explanation: The Party for the Animals supports making long-term vacant buildings habitable. This can often be done well without municipal subsidies.

112771.explanation: We are against mandatory work obligations attached to benefits. This can displace existing jobs and thus increase unemployment. It can also limit people in following a study or their opportunities to apply for jobs. Finally, we consider self-determination to be very important.

112772.explanation: Many people’s wallets are already empty before the end of the month. Dordrecht can and must do more to alleviate and prevent poverty.

112775.explanation: The introduction of a basic income creates more equality. The municipality informs the national government, which is responsible, that Dordrecht wants a pilot with a basic income.

112776.explanation: The municipality provides enough charging points with green electricity for electric vehicles and vessels. These are integrated into the existing streetscape where possible, for example, in the ground or lampposts.

112781.explanation: We want care that fits the person. Care providers must take into account the background and identity of those seeking help. Selecting individual care providers can encourage discrimination. Therefore, we are not in favor of that.

112785.explanation: Dordrecht has allocated a huge area to businesses, including the Dordtse Kil IV, a historical mistake. When businesses want to expand, existing space is better used. The municipality opposes urban sprawl and rejects large-scale distribution centers and data centers.

112786.explanation: The municipality meets the housing target for status holders and opposes the bill that removes priority for status holders for social housing. If the law is nevertheless adopted, Dordrecht will create a local priority scheme for status holders.

112788.explanation: No solar parks will be built in nature. The installation of solar panels will use a solar ladder that determines the order of construction. Agricultural land is meant for plant-based food production and nature development, not for solar meadows.

112791.explanation: In the past 10 years, the number of social housing units in Dordrecht has only decreased. More affordable homes will be built: at least 30% of the homes will be social rent.

112794.explanation: Look at the Spuiboulevard on a Saturday and you see: Dordt is gridlocked. Dordt can be so much cleaner, more beautiful, and healthier if we change how we travel. Paid parking will be introduced throughout the municipality, and second and subsequent parking permits will become much more expensive.

112795.explanation: Dordt can be so much cleaner, more beautiful, and healthier if we change how we travel. The car is no longer in charge; there will be more space for pedestrians, cyclists, and public transport. For greenery and playing children.

112796.explanation: The city center will become low-traffic. Dordrecht chooses car-free streets where possible, both in the city center and beyond.

112797.explanation: The Party for the Animals wants to solve the housing shortage sustainably. We keep our wide polders intact and therefore build within the city limits. There is enough space there, for example, on the Maasterras and Stadswerven.

112799.explanation: There are enough coffeeshops in Dordrecht. The Party for the Animals supports better distribution across the city. The municipality investigates the possibility of drive-through coffeeshops in industrial areas to reduce nuisance from existing coffeeshops.

112800.explanation: The location of a hospital must meet various requirements and conditions. Moreover, it is not possible to keep a hospital open while demolishing and rebuilding the building at the same time. We therefore do not want to impose this requirement on the hospital.

112801.explanation: Public transport must be free for people with low incomes. For everyone, public transport must remain safe, reliable, frequent, affordable, and accessible.

112802.explanation: As long as shops do not cause nuisance, store owners should have the free choice to open their doors on Sunday mornings.

112803.explanation: What we throw away can often still be turned into something useful. Dordrecht therefore takes measures to reduce residual waste. The municipality focuses on separating waste, reducing waste, and high-quality waste processing.

112805.explanation: Dordrecht invests in sustainable, high-quality sports facilities for Dordrecht sports clubs. However, money can only be spent once. Paid (top) sports must stand on its own feet.

112747.explanation: Referendums must not undermine local democracy but strengthen it. Denk is not against referendums but wants to use them carefully, fairly, and inclusively. Citizens must be honestly, fully, and accessibly informed in advance, especially on major social or political issues.

112748.explanation: Denk Dordrecht supports citizen involvement, but sports facilities must remain accessible and affordable for everyone. Taking over by citizens can offer opportunities but also carries risks such as higher costs and inequality between neighborhoods.

112750.explanation: Cafes and restaurants should have more freedom to decide for themselves.

112751.explanation: The municipality plays a crucial role in improving the reception and integration of refugees. Denk believes our municipality must also take responsibility.

112755.explanation: Basic facilities must be affordable or free. That contributes to clean, livable neighborhoods and helps prevent illegal dumping.

112756.explanation: Rules must be clear and simple. No unnecessary bureaucracy around parties and festivals. Organizers, especially neighborhood initiatives and small festivals, should not get bogged down in paperwork. Safety and livability remain important without excessive permit requirements.

112758.explanation: Yes, membership fees can be collected via the Dordtpass. This makes participation in sports, culture, and associations accessible and affordable for everyone, regardless of income.

112761.explanation: The municipality finances workshops, music lessons, and art programs in disadvantaged neighborhoods.

112765.explanation: Camera surveillance can contribute to safety and crime detection, but only under strict conditions. It is used targeted and temporarily in problem areas with full protection of privacy and fundamental rights. Camera surveillance is supplementary and never a replacement for human supervision.

112766.explanation: The focus of the public order officers (boa's) should be on prevention, de-escalation, and community-oriented work.

112769.explanation: By transforming office buildings, housing can be created for specific groups, such as young people, starters, the elderly, status holders, and people with disabilities.

112771.explanation: Social assistance should be a safety net, not a punishment. Programs should be voluntary, appropriate, and focused on development. We oppose mandatory counter-performances.

112772.explanation: Increases in levies that affect ordinary residents must be prevented, especially for low and middle incomes. Remissions for minimum income earners are maintained and increased where necessary.

112775.explanation: We are open to innovative solutions to reduce poverty and financial insecurity. A pilot with a basic income can provide insight into the effects on residents' well-being, independence, and social participation. It is a way to increase livelihood security without stigmatization.

112776.explanation: Denk Dordrecht considers sustainable mobility important. More parking spaces as charging spots must be created, but not at the expense of regular parking spaces. This way, we promote electric driving while keeping the city accessible for all residents.

112781.explanation: Culturally sensitive care remains a top priority. In the municipality's care contracts, cultural sensitivity is a requirement and training in cultural sensitivity is promoted.

112786.explanation: The municipality of Dordrecht meets the statutory requirements regarding the housing of status holders.

112791.explanation: Denk Dordrecht wants the municipality to take the lead in housing construction with a focus on affordable housing: 40% social, 40% affordable mid-rent/ownership, and 20% higher segment.

112794.explanation: We want parking fees to be lowered.

112795.explanation: Denk Dordrecht raises the parking standard for new construction to 1.5 spaces per dwelling and preserves existing parking spaces. This keeps neighborhoods accessible and livable for residents, visitors, and entrepreneurs, without sacrificing parking spots.

112797.explanation: Denk Dordrecht wants to realize affordable social rental and owner-occupied homes in the polder, with small-scale, nature-inclusive construction that preserves the green character and biodiversity, so that living and nature remain in balance.

112803.explanation: Denk Dordrecht supports waste separation. We advocate for an accessible and user-friendly waste policy. Therefore, we choose open underground waste containers without a card system. Disposing of waste is a basic service and must be simple, accessible, and available to everyone.

112805.explanation: There must be an end to decades of uncertainty about the FC Dordrecht stadium. A new stadium must be built as soon as possible.

112747.explanation: We do not rule out a consultative referendum on important issues for the city.

112748.explanation: The municipality is always responsible for the finances of its own buildings.

112750.explanation: And shops... as long as the entrepreneur acts responsibly to prevent nuisance to the immediate surroundings, then everyone may be open 24 hours a day according to us.

112751.explanation: At this moment, there is no asylum reception except for sheltering Ukrainians. If this occurs in the future, we oppose it.

112755.explanation: For most people, it is no problem to bring their own bulky waste; they can do so at a time convenient for them. Additionally, collection moments are organized in neighborhoods for those without transport to the recycling center.

112756.explanation: Soon you might not even be allowed to talk on your balcony or in your garden. No, as it is now, it is fine.

112758.explanation: We find it important that everyone gets enough exercise. For children of low-income families, we have a Dordtpas where a sports credit can be set up. If adults cannot afford a membership, we must look for a solution, possibly in cooperation with clubs.

112761.explanation: Music is important for the development of all children.

112765.explanation: It is important that footage is regularly reviewed and immediate action is taken if the situation requires it.

112766.explanation: Public order officers are not police officers; they have followed a different training and are thus equipped with, among other things, a baton. In our view, this would only be acceptable if there is training on how and especially when to use such a baton, but preferably not.

112769.explanation: The owners can pay for this themselves, but the municipality must allow it and not see objections everywhere.

112771.explanation: And if it concerns volunteer work, in our view they may receive a volunteer allowance without having deductions made.

112772.explanation: However, we may well question whether in the future it is better to charge something with, for example, a scale. There should be an awareness that nothing is free.

112775.explanation: This is something that must come from national politics and not from local government. Besides, we believe that introducing a basic income will make everything more expensive, and we want to prevent that.

112776.explanation: There are still many more people without than with an electric car. The first group should also give up their car. Possibly make it easier to install a connection at home.

112781.explanation: Which does not mean that care can be refused if that fails. So, it is checked if someone suits you, and if not, someone who can provide the right care will be assigned; that is more important.

112785.explanation: In our opinion, the current industrial estates offer sufficient space for current and future companies. Some sites should be reorganized.

112786.explanation: No, we believe they should simply participate like other home seekers in the existing system.

112788.explanation: If someone wants to install solar panels on their own land, that must be possible.

112791.explanation: Building must be for everyone, so that there are enough opportunities for people to move up.

112794.explanation: We find the current rates too high and strive for cheaper parking.

112795.explanation: We can pretend that no one drives a car anymore but that is not realistic. Many young people still living at home because there is no house often already have their own car, which they find increasingly difficult to get rid of. This leads to nuisance in undesirable places, which is less with more parking spaces.

112796.explanation: We believe everyone should be able to reach their home by car, just as visitors or caregivers should be able to pick up or drop someone off by car.

112797.explanation: There are several opportunities in the polder to build homes with respect for the environment. We think specifically of Nieuw Dubbeldam and the Zuidpolder.

112799.explanation: If coffeeshops comply with the rules, then there is no problem with them being located in the city. If an entrepreneur wants to move to an industrial area, that should also be possible.

112800.explanation: The ASz consults with the municipality about the best location for the new hospital. It is up to the hospital itself to make its own choice within the possibilities available.

112801.explanation: Free bus rides cost the municipality a lot of money, while people aged 65 and over can travel with a 34% discount, which we believe is sufficient.

112802.explanation: The municipality should allow shops to be open 24 hours a day, including Sundays starting at 8 a.m. This way, entrepreneurs can decide when their shop is open to better cater to their target audience.

112803.explanation: We believe that waste separation afterwards (nascheiding) is more realistic in a city like Dordrecht. It is also clearer for residents.

112805.explanation: As far as we are concerned, the municipality does not need to contribute financially, but we do think the municipality can provide a guarantee. This would make it easier for the FC to take out a loan for the (re)construction of the stadium.

112747.explanation: That is true democracy On Our Island. We have been advocating for this for years.

112748.explanation: Provided that they are professionally supervised.

112750.explanation: Provided that this does not cause nuisance. Our idea is to start with a trial first.

112751.explanation: That is a matter for the national government. Shelter is allowed, but then spread out and on a smaller scale in Dordrecht.

112755.explanation: That is what we have been advocating for years. Initiated by On Our Island, the recycling center (for bulky waste) will also be open on Sundays in Dordrecht.

112756.explanation: Dordrecht has grown through events. This can have a counterproductive effect for the sector. However, hearing damage must be prevented by conducting measurements during a festival.

112758.explanation: Sports should be accessible to everyone.

112761.explanation: Music should return to schools. It is an important skill for students.

112765.explanation: Especially at hotspots, so that safety is somewhat improved.

112769.explanation: We would like that, but the municipality does not have the financial means. Additionally, there are also national subsidies for transformation and energy measures.

112771.explanation: Everyone who can contribute according to their 'capacity' is actively invited to do so. There are so many associations looking for volunteers and unable to find them.

112772.explanation: More consideration should be given to the financial capacity of Dordrecht residents.

112775.explanation: There is already a Participation Act benefit (social assistance).

112776.explanation: Encourage more home charging. That is also much cheaper. Home charging is possible in Dordrecht. On Our Island has advocated this.

112781.explanation: The municipality has no role here. That is up to the Dordrecht resident themselves.

112785.explanation: There is no more room for expansion of industrial areas. Existing sites, such as Dordtse Kil, must be restructured according to current and future economic demands.

112786.explanation: Every Dordrecht resident is treated the same way in the distribution/allocation of housing.

112788.explanation: Agricultural land is important for crops/food with a low footprint. On Our Island supports food from local soil.

112791.explanation: We have an enormous demand for 'affordable' social rental and owner-occupied homes.

112794.explanation: Paid parking has become a cash cow for the municipality. Prices have risen enormously, making accessibility to, among others, the city center almost impossible. Also, paid parking does not provide 'extra' parking spaces.

112795.explanation: Especially on the edges of neighborhoods, parking spaces should be created that can possibly be monitored, so that company vehicles can also be parked there.

112796.explanation: Absolutely not. Many Dordrecht residents depend on cars to reach the city center. Partly because we do not have a well-functioning city bus, accessibility to the city center by car remains important.

112797.explanation: Not directly in the polder, but the option to keep certain areas, such as behind Stevensweg, open for housing development. However, this must be done in good consultation with local residents.

112799.explanation: Industrial areas are for industry. Enforcing any nuisance will be very difficult on industrial sites.

112800.explanation: The hospital remains in Dordrecht and is undergoing significant renewal, with new construction plans.

112801.explanation: On Our Island wants a free city bus for all Dordrecht residents.

112802.explanation: This is the entrepreneur's freedom. In a 24-hour economy, the entrepreneur should decide this themselves.

112803.explanation: Four garbage bins in the garden are not an enhancement of the streetscape. On Our Island has advocated for waste separation afterwards for years. There are various municipalities where this works very successfully.

112805.explanation: The municipality invests in infrastructure around the stadium and the FC invests in a new stadium.

112747.explanation: The SP considers the instrument of a binding referendum very important, not only nationally but also locally. It invites residents to participate in political decision-making and puts a topic on the agenda.

112748.explanation: We believe that the municipality is primarily responsible for facilities such as sports halls to make the necessary investments and ensure fair use by various clubs.

112750.explanation: Maximum freedom for entrepreneurs in this sounds nice, but there is a risk that smaller cafes and restaurants with fewer staff will no longer be able to compete with larger chains. A level playing field is therefore important. This also applies to shops.

112751.explanation: Dordrecht will receive a large asylum seeker center (azc), which we find reasonable as a medium-sized municipality. With this, we have taken our responsibility to accommodate refugees. The SP is against additional azcs in the future.

112755.explanation: The SP wants to make it possible again to have bulky waste collected for free on fixed days. This is good for the livability of neighborhoods. We do not support individual collection each time at ever-increasing costs for residents.

112756.explanation: At this moment, we see no urgent reason to reduce the noise level. In our opinion, events are well supervised in this regard.

112758.explanation: For people who can barely make ends meet, there are good arrangements, such as the Dordtpas or special social assistance. This can be used for sports but also for other matters.

112761.explanation: Music lessons should be a standard part of primary school education, so if the statement is meant in this way, we agree.

112765.explanation: Camera surveillance sounds appealing but ultimately is not a solution for nuisance and crime. Quick responses to reports, visible enforcement officers in the neighborhood, and neighborhood apps are better solutions for a safer city.

112766.explanation: We really leave this to the enforcement officers themselves. If they believe they need more options to defend themselves, we will pay attention to that.

112769.explanation: The housing shortage is so severe that offices that have been empty for too long and have no quick prospect of new business use can be converted into homes much faster. The municipality must play an important role in this.

112771.explanation: The SP advocates for municipal jobs, work that must be done by the government or by companies collaborating with the government. Think of transportation for target groups, which is currently lacking. People on welfare are eligible for this.

112772.explanation: For people who are really in financial trouble, this possibility must remain. We do not want people to fall into high debts because of the municipality. Ultimately, this is undesirable for both the municipality and the resident.

112775.explanation: The SP is not in favor of a basic income. A job with good conditions and a fair salary are the best ways to secure a good income. If people really cannot work, there are good arrangements.

112776.explanation: A parking space reserved for electric cars takes up space that cannot be used elsewhere. There are more and more electric cars, which we do not oppose. We prefer options for charging at home or work rather than a separate location.

112781.explanation: When this is an option, it can be facilitated for people. However, we must also acknowledge that there are staff shortages in healthcare and that this might not always be possible.

112785.explanation: We are in a major housing crisis and at the same time we see no need to drastically expand business parks; there is more than enough space for large-scale business activity in Dordrecht. We prefer to use space for housing and preserve greenery.

112786.explanation: Genuine refugees with a status must have housing. It is undesirable to let people stay in an asylum seekers' center for many years. But many starters also search for social rental housing. Therefore, we are greatly expanding the construction of affordable homes.

112788.explanation: There are still many flat roofs where solar panels can be installed. There are also small patches of land that cannot be used (leftover spots) where panels can be placed. So this does not need to be on agricultural land.

112791.explanation: 30% is really the minimum; according to the SP, this percentage may be higher. We have a duty to truly offer homes to youth and starters.

112794.explanation: A reasonable rate for parking garages and street parking is justifiable. Parking does not need to be free everywhere. However, we reject huge rate increases. It is therefore important that parking garages remain municipal property and not owned by commercial parties.

112795.explanation: Neighborhoods must be designed so that there is a reasonable number of parking spaces compared to the number of homes. Adding even more parking spaces on top harms the space we have. We also want to preserve trees, benches, playgrounds, and so on.

112796.explanation: The Old City Center can become more car-reduced and car-free. We prefer to designate certain areas as pedestrian zones. This makes a city more attractive to stay in. We will provide extra parking garages around the city center.

112797.explanation: In our view, building in the polder is not yet urgent in the coming years. However, we are open to certain possibilities in the future.

112799.explanation: By relocating all coffeeshops, we believe this will encourage drug-related crime. The shops must comply with certain rules but do not need to be moved out of the city center.

112800.explanation: We prefer to wait for concrete plans before responding to this.

112801.explanation: Older people sometimes do not want or dare to get in the car anymore, and to prevent mobility problems for them, we think it is a good idea if they can use the bus for free. This may also reduce traffic congestion on our roads.

112802.explanation: Further relaxation of opening hours mainly benefits large chains that can attract more staff. We are mindful of the small entrepreneur. A level playing field gives them a better chance of a successful business.

112803.explanation: Separating waste remains a personal choice that we will continue to offer. However, we see more in a system of post-sorting. This makes it easier for people to dispose of their waste.

112805.explanation: FC Dordrecht is a commercial party and must ensure good operation of a new facility through sponsorships and other income. It is not appropriate for tax money to be used here.

112747.explanation: Residents are involved far too little in important decisions. If an important decision lends itself to a referendum, PVV Dordrecht is a strong advocate for this.

112748.explanation: It entirely depends on the circumstances, wishes, and agreements with residents whether municipal management could be taken over.

112750.explanation: Cafés and restaurants should determine their own opening hours, provided there is no (or increased) nuisance for neighbors.

112751.explanation: PVV Dordrecht expects the college of Mayor and Aldermen to at least lobby the State to repeal the Distribution Act as soon as possible and to take measures to minimize the influx of asylum seekers. The asylum seekers' center should be repurposed as soon as possible.

112755.explanation: Nothing is free; this is paid for from collective funds. It is in the collective interest that the streets remain clean and that residents report when they want to dispose of bulky waste so it can be collected.

112756.explanation: It is unclear whether this statement refers to sound standards for visitors or for residents who experience nuisance. Also, the number of festivals and parties at the same location and the surrounding area influence the answer to this question.

112758.explanation: This statement is too general. Personal circumstances are leading. Automatically giving every adult on welfare a sports club membership is not wise. Children of parents on welfare should never be restricted in their opportunity to play sports.

112761.explanation: Every primary school child should be given the opportunity to participate in music lessons. However, this does not automatically mean that every primary school child, for example those from wealthy parents, should qualify for subsidized music lessons.

112765.explanation: To support enforcement officers and police, it is (unfortunately) necessary to have effective camera surveillance in some places.

112766.explanation: Enforcement officers who go out on the streets for us Dordrecht residents every day must be able to defend themselves fully in dangerous situations. To be properly able to defend themselves, acquiring a baton must be preceded by a training program.

112769.explanation: This statement is too general. It entirely depends on the added value of the subsidy, the conditions agreed upon, and whether it actually leads to faster conversion of empty offices into homes.

112771.explanation: It entirely depends on the (personal) circumstances. The starting point must always be that someone on welfare should be available and willing to perform socially useful work if the (personal) circumstances allow.

112772.explanation: It entirely depends on the (personal) circumstances. The starting point must always be that everyone who can should contribute financially to the municipality.

112775.explanation: Residents should, in principle, be helped to provide for their own livelihood. If this proves impossible and meaningful social work is also not feasible, PVV Dordrecht is open to considering whether a separate arrangement can be made for that group.

112776.explanation: Most parking spaces for charging points are already often vacant the majority of the time. It also disadvantages motorists without an electric car. The ever-increasing charging speeds of new generations of electric cars will quickly make even more charging points unnecessary.

112781.explanation: Providing or receiving care should not depend on religion, culture, or belief.

112785.explanation: It entirely depends on where and to what extent there is a need for more space. The starting point is to be cautious, considering the still available space and spatial choices that need to be made.

112786.explanation: The only people who should have priority for a social rental home in Dordrecht are those born and raised in Dordrecht.

112788.explanation: Solar panels should only be installed on roofs, never on the ground. PVV Dordrecht also supports investigating whether existing land-based solar panels can be relocated to roofs, so that that land can be used for other purposes again.

112791.explanation: You can’t live in percentages. PVV Dordrecht therefore mainly wants affordable owner-occupied and rental homes to be built quickly without getting caught up in a discussion over a percentage more or less in a certain category.

112794.explanation: In locations where the municipality of Dordrecht controls the pricing, a more car-friendly policy must be pursued.

112795.explanation: PVV Dordrecht is open to reorganizing a neighborhood where possible (and desired by residents!) if this can create more parking spaces.

112796.explanation: The center of Dordrecht must remain as accessible as possible for motorists.

112797.explanation: Population growth has enormously increased due to the failing migration policy of the national government. The municipal executive must urge the national government to stop the influx, so that there will never be a need to consider sacrificing Dordrecht's polder landscape for small-scale housing.

112799.explanation: When a coffeeshop causes nuisance, relocating it often only shifts the nuisance elsewhere. Solving the nuisance should therefore be the main focus. PVV Dordrecht is open to relocation if it can contribute to resolving the problem.

112800.explanation: As far as the municipality of Dordrecht can influence the location of the new building, the municipal executive should coordinate primarily with the hospital on how the new building can be used most efficiently.

112801.explanation: PVV Dordrecht wants to keep this limited to Dordrecht AOW pensioners with a Dordtpas.

112802.explanation: Shopkeepers should be free to determine their own opening hours, provided no nuisance is caused to local residents.

112803.explanation: Waste streams where post-separation can be applied are preferred.

112805.explanation: Subsidies to make football activities possible for a professional football club are not justifiable at this time given the enormous challenges and related expenses to maintain liveability in Dordrecht. A loan with favorable conditions is, depending on the spending purpose, negotiable.

112747.explanation: For D66, the opinion of Dordrecht residents is very important. We introduce neighborhood referendums, expand neighborhood budgets, and revive the Dordrecht Youth Council. Your opinion counts!

112748.explanation: D66 embraces initiatives and ideas from residents. Involved Dordtenaren carry the city and deserve space.

112750.explanation: D66 supports a vibrant city. The municipality should make it possible for cafes and restaurants to extend their opening hours. That better fits entrepreneurs’ needs and keeps the city lively.

112751.explanation: D66 supports a fair distribution of asylum seekers across the country. As a city, we take our responsibility. We do this at reception locations where everyone learns the language and participates from day one. We ensure the safety of asylum seekers and the neighborhood.

112755.explanation: For D66, everyone is responsible for a clean city. Bulky waste can be delivered free of charge at the recycling yard. A fair fee applies for bulky waste collection.

112756.explanation: D66 supports a vibrant city. There must be a good balance between a pleasant living environment and lively events.

112758.explanation: For D66, sports are important. We invest in expanding and making sports facilities more sustainable, inclusive playgrounds, and outdoor amenities. This way, everyone can use local facilities for free. Children retain a sports budget via the Dordtpas.

112761.explanation: D66 is the education party. Music education contributes to broad development. This is good for our children and the future of the cultural sector.

112765.explanation: For D66, privacy is important. Therefore, we are cautious about expanding camera surveillance. We always keep installation of cameras as an option when the situation demands it.

112766.explanation: D66 believes a police officer should have a baton. We support a clear distinction in tasks between the police and enforcement officers.

112769.explanation: D66’s highest priority is solving the housing shortage. We advocate for a vacancy fine and abolishing rules. For tailor-made solutions, such as monumental buildings, the municipality targets subsidies to convert them into homes.

112771.explanation: D66 wants to encourage people on welfare benefits to contribute socially. It should be easier to follow education, provide informal care, or do volunteer work. This reduces the distance to the labor market.

112772.explanation: D66 wants to help people out of poverty. By waiving taxes, people with low incomes get more financial breathing space and we prevent a mountain of debts.

112775.explanation: D66 supports an effective approach to poverty policy. An expensive and risky basic income experiment is not a good way to spend public money.

112776.explanation: D66 believes the municipality has a facilitating role to ensure sufficient charging stations in the city. Dordrecht is behind in installing charging facilities. A shortage of charging points must not be a barrier to switching to electric driving in Dordrecht.

112781.explanation: D66 is an inclusive party and stands for good care. Refusing a healthcare provider based on their religion, culture, or belief does not fit in our free society. Moreover, the municipality does not actively intervene in this.

112785.explanation: D66 views business activity on our island positively. If there is a need for expansion, we are willing to support it, for example near the Dordtse Kil.

112786.explanation: D66’s highest priority is solving the housing shortage. With a home, status holders can work and integrate more quickly. We build more and remove rules so every resident of Dordrecht can find a home.

112788.explanation: D66 is a strong supporter of solar energy. Therefore, we choose to invest in solar panels on roofs. Space on our island is scarce and can be better utilized. A complete ban goes too far. On private land, the owner decides how to use their land.

112791.explanation: D66 sees a major challenge for the municipality to catch up on the backlog of social rental housing. We do not see a fixed 30% quota as a solution. We adjust the building mix based on the needs of people who work and live in Dordrecht.

112794.explanation: D66 believes parking revenue should not be used to fill budget gaps. The center must remain attractive to visit. We support keeping current rates.

112795.explanation: D66 is the party for greenery in the streets. That enhances liveability and prevents extreme heat in summer. Parking and greenery do not exclude each other. We lay parking lots with grass pavers and provide sufficient shade with trees.

112796.explanation: D66 supports a car-reduced inner city. That makes our city clean, safe, and liveable. We provide sufficient parking on the edges of the center. The inner city remains accessible by car for entrepreneurs, suppliers, and residents.

112797.explanation: D66’s highest priority is solving the housing shortage. Building within the city is our preference, and new construction outside the center primarily goes upwards. Expansion at the city edges is an option for us under conditions.

112799.explanation: D66 supports responsible soft drug use. The traffic nuisance caused by coffee shops must be limited. Coffee shops have their own responsibility in this. In case of persistent nuisance, we will look into solutions such as a drive-in coffee shop at the city outskirts.

112800.explanation: D66 considers good healthcare important. The Albert Schweitzer Hospital is centrally located and easily accessible for everyone in our city and beyond. It is important that the hospital can continue to provide good and accessible care.

112801.explanation: Mobility is important to D66. Elderly people must be able to travel from A to B safely and comfortably. We advocate for free public transport for people with low incomes, including elderly with low incomes. Allowing all elderly to travel for free is not a good use of communal funds.

112802.explanation: D66 supports a vibrant city. Businesses and entrepreneurs who need it should be allowed to open earlier on Sundays.

112803.explanation: D66 believes everyone is responsible for a clean city. By separating and recycling waste, we contribute to this goal. Post-separation is a logical choice if HVC indicates it can separate waste better than residents. That is not currently the case.

112805.explanation: D66 Dordrecht is a big supporter of FC Dordrecht. We do not believe it is the municipality's task to ensure a new stadium. The municipality should assist with renovation of the Krommedijk, but this does not require large amounts of taxpayer money.

112747.explanation: Everyone is allowed to express and voice their opinion.

112748.explanation: Managing a sports hall requires knowledge and organizational skills. If clear rules are established for the use and maintenance of the sports hall, there are no obstacles and it can be managed by anyone.

112750.explanation: Complete freedom can cause problems, but more room within clear frameworks (for example per area or with input from residents) could be a good balance between entrepreneurship and livability.

112751.explanation: Everyone deserves a chance, even an equal chance. That is what the Party for the Rule of Law stands for.

112755.explanation: Free collection leads to more costs and therefore higher taxes. It also results in less litter but possibly more waste while reuse is still possible. Nevertheless, it is very convenient for people without their own transport.

112756.explanation: As long as it does not pose a risk of hearing damage, people can take care of themselves when it comes to protection.

112758.explanation: Everyone must have the opportunity to participate in sports. People receiving social assistance may need help, and the municipality can provide that assistance.

112761.explanation: Music is important for social development. Every interested child should have the opportunity to learn to make music.

112765.explanation: More supervision means more safety. Incidents can be anticipated more quickly.

112766.explanation: Ensuring safety does not always require violence. In risk areas and/or moments, specific training and clear protocols can make a significant difference regarding carrying a baton.

112769.explanation: Developers are often willing to make large investments. Usually, this is easier if a subsidy can be linked. Less vacancy and more homes address housing shortages.

112771.explanation: A voluntary and appropriate counter-performance, tailored to capacity and talents, can be effective. The focus should be on development and participation, not punishment or control.

112772.explanation: It increases poverty and debts. Partial waivers or income-dependent rates can help.

112775.explanation: A pilot of basic income in Dordrecht can provide valuable insights but must be set up carefully, affordably, and within legal possibilities. It costs the municipality a lot of money. We have not yet formed a definitive position on this matter. We want to be well informed about the consequences and possibilities before deciding whether we are for or against it.

112776.explanation: More reserved parking spaces cause more parking stress. There are sufficient charging points, among others at fuel stations.

112781.explanation: Choice freedom in healthcare is important, but 'always' is not realistic. The aim should be to accommodate this as much as possible without compromising accessibility and quality of care.

112785.explanation: More entrepreneurs/businesses mean more jobs and are good for social growth.

112786.explanation: It would help achieve fast integration; legal and moral responsibility applies here. It also increases societal tensions amid the existing housing shortage. Distribution and additional housing construction can solve this problem.

112788.explanation: Sustainability is important, and there are still many roofs available for solar panels. Agricultural land is important for growing crops.

112791.explanation: More social rental housing is necessary. Let us take the lead in Dordrecht.

112794.explanation: Lower rates can lead to more shopping visits. Lower rates can also lead to more car traffic and less bicycle traffic. Cheaper parking in the suburbs can be encouraged.

112795.explanation: In Dordrecht, more and more households have one or more cars, and young people live at home longer, resulting in multiple cars per home more often. The number of parking spaces has not increased accordingly. This increases parking pressure in neighborhoods.

112796.explanation: Car-free zones provide more safety for pedestrians and cyclists and are better for the environment. However, shops become less accessible, parking nuisance shifts to other neighborhoods, and people with mobility difficulties are disadvantaged.

112797.explanation: It helps solve housing shortages. Pressure on the city is reduced. The downside is that there will be more car traffic and nature will be affected.

112799.explanation: Enforcement is easier in the city center, but nuisance can be more present. Moving a coffee shop could encourage street dealing, potentially leading to more crime.

112800.explanation: The municipality can choose the current location if research shows that a good, accessible, and sustainable hospital can also be located there in the future. It is important that a fixed location does not come at the expense of the quality of care.

112801.explanation: Provide older people with more opportunities to remain independent.

112802.explanation: Entrepreneurial freedom can lead to more targeted store openings.

112803.explanation: It is better for the environment but not feasible for everyone. Elderly or people with small homes may not be able to accommodate this.

112805.explanation: If the stadium can be used for neighborhood events and youth activities, it is a good investment. A new stadium will attract more visitors. It is unfair to other sports clubs that do not receive support.

112747.explanation: We live in a parliamentary democracy where the population elects representatives. A representative can indeed consult voters, but the democratic decision-making process is different than simply holding a referendum. A referendum offers people only two choices: yes or no.

112748.explanation: Activities for residents may also be organized by residents themselves, so no objection if residents want to manage this themselves.

112750.explanation: Longer opening hours should be possible. Having complete freedom to decide now would not be workable.

112751.explanation: Asylum seeker centers are waiting rooms to accommodate people who should move on as soon as possible. Ultimately, one would want to help in a more humane way than these 'waiting rooms.' We see this really as an emergency solution, but not the solution to the problem.

112755.explanation: Only where no other option is possible and then for a small fee.

112756.explanation: With even stricter rules, it only becomes more difficult for organizers. We want less regulatory pressure.

112758.explanation: The word 'must' causes unease. The municipality may certainly encourage this and offer compensation for people with a low income. Simply paying the membership fee is too easy. It could work as an incentive to reimburse a large part upon seeing certain effort.

112761.explanation: Artistic and creative development for children is very important and every child should have the opportunity to participate.

112765.explanation: There is already camera surveillance. Increasing it is not a priority.

112766.explanation: If a baton ensures that the public enforcement officer feels safer because he/she can defend themselves in certain situations, it certainly has added value.

112769.explanation: If a good business case is made and the environmental permit is logical, then subsidy is not needed because there are enough investors who can get started with this. The limitation is often the regulations, not the availability of financial resources.

112771.explanation: Again here the word 'must'. People are often on social assistance for a reason. Yes, there are people who abuse the system, but often there are underlying problems that are not seen. So in exchange for benefits, personal development could be encouraged to offer perspective.

112772.explanation: If we all live somewhere, then we all contribute to society. It should be that the basic elements of a society are equal. The point is that the government takes on more and more and that costs money. Everyone, including people with a low income, must pay taxes. Otherwise, in theory, everyone could live on a minimum benefit and no one would pay taxes anymore. Is that the desire?

112775.explanation: Less government interference regarding the income of the population.

112776.explanation: When designating parking spaces for electric vehicles, no additional places are added for regular vehicles. This would therefore result in even fewer parking spots, which are already in short supply. Gas stations also have charging points, just like regular refueling.

112781.explanation: When we talk about an inclusive society, it should be about the quality of care here, where care is for everyone regardless of religion, race, or anything else.

112785.explanation: There is more need for housing than extra business space, but if necessary and space permits, we will not stand in the way.

112786.explanation: Due to the tight housing market, there is scarcity for everyone and therefore no positive discrimination.

112788.explanation: We prefer to preserve agriculture and therefore oppose solar panels.

112791.explanation: There should be a mix of needs considered. Not everyone is entitled to social housing but cannot buy or pay very high rents. We thus miss a part of the market with too much focus on 30% social housing.

112794.explanation: With the remark that everything costs money, so you will have to make different choices across the board.

112795.explanation: Preservation of what exists. Sometimes there are insufficient parking spots, but this is often because older children continue living at home due to lack of available housing. More parking spots is therefore symptom treatment, not solving the problem.

112796.explanation: The car-free zone is already ample. More promotion of low-car areas/moments is possible if surrounding roads are easily accessible and flow well.

112797.explanation: If Dordrecht wants to grow into a municipality of 140,000, unpopular choices must be made. One affects the other. Population growth requires expansion, and urban development is still possible but too expensive and too slow.

112799.explanation: The effect of a coffeeshop in the city is that cars quickly drive in and out and park briefly on the sidewalk. Customers do not go there to sit but make a targeted visit and then leave. That means it can easily be located in an outlying area.

112800.explanation: The hospital will need to be advised by spatial planning experts.

112801.explanation: It would now be more logical to raise the age limit to 67+ and free entry is often unnecessary, but a discount is acceptable.

112802.explanation: A decision was recently made on a broader store opening hours regulation that responded to the needs of residents and visitors. Extending Sunday hours to 8:00 a.m. was not desired and therefore has no added value.

112803.explanation: Source separation causes many problems, leading to the desire to better investigate post-separation.

112805.explanation: This is not about co-paying. It is about creating the conditions that make it possible to build a new stadium. This concerns a commercial club capable of closing network and sponsorship deals, and the municipality may facilitate and support this.

statements.title: The municipality should allow shops to open at 8 a.m. on Sundays.
Noise standards at festivals and parties must be made stricter.
The residents of Dordrecht should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Dordrecht moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: There should be more camera surveillance in Dordrecht.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: The municipality should urge the Albert Schweitzer Hospital to build its new building on the same site.
The asylum reception center in our municipality should be closed as soon as possible.
